concerns.text: T012 permits falling back to 'nilearn CPU methods' for preprocessing. This violates FR-002 and Constitution Principle VI which mandate 'FSL or AFNI' pipelines to ensure version-locked preprocessing integrity. Allowing a semantic fallback to a different toolchain weakens the reproducibility constraint.

T013 schedules immediate deletion of raw NIfTI files after preprocessing. This violates Constitution Principle III ('Raw data is preserved unchanged') and Principle I (Reproducibility), as re-running the pipeline from a fresh state would fail without the source raw data. The task silently relaxes the data hygiene constraint.

T028 implements sensitivity sweeps for motion and effect thresholds but omits the sweep for the primary statistical significance threshold (p-value) or FDR parameters. SC-005 requires a sensitivity analysis for 'any decision cutoff'. This task delivers a partial solution that falls short of the acceptance criterion.

T006 implements exclusion logic for high-motion subjects but lacks the verification step to ensure the remaining sample size meets the n ≥ 20 threshold required by SC-004. The task allows the pipeline to proceed with insufficient statistical power, violating the success criterion.

T033 generates a report framing findings as 'associational/causal' based on randomization status. The spec (Assumptions) explicitly defines the design as a 'within-subject longitudinal study' (causal potential). This task introduces a dynamic downgrade mechanism not present in the spec, creating a semantic drift in the inference claim.
